commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Instruct. The original is translated try, ver. 4, and chap. ii. 22.
And all. Hebrew, "as many of Israel as had not "(Haydock)
Those who had served under Josue, were so strongly impressed with a sense of the divine power and severity, that they never forgot them: but there was danger lest their children should grow careless, if they were suffered to enjoy a constant state of prosperity. Virtue or power is made perfect in infirmity, 2 Corinthians xii. 9. (Calmet)
He that hath been experienced in many things, multiplieth prudence, Ecclesiasticus xxxiv. 10.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
